name: Pure carry (real rate differential)
source: Lustig, Roussanov, Verdelhan 2011, Common Risk Factors in Currency Markets (RFS)
rationale: High-rate currencies historically earn excess returns over low-rate ones, compensating for crash risk. The most-documented FX factor, 40+ years.

name: Carry with momentum confirmation
source: Lustig-Verdelhan 2011 + Menkhoff et al 2012b momentum overlay
rationale: Carry entries filtered by 63-day price momentum agreement — avoids entering carry into adverse trends (a partial crash-risk mitigant).

name: IRP-deviation mean reversion (value)
source: Asness, Moskowitz, Pedersen 2013, Value and Momentum Everywhere (JF)
rationale: Currencies cheap vs covered-interest-parity fair value mean-revert. Value factor; classically low/negatively correlated with carry and momentum.

name: Carry-tilted blend (70/30 rate/IRP)
source: Composite — Lustig-Verdelhan carry + AMP value diversification
rationale: Carry-dominant blend with a value tilt to dampen carry crash exposure. Tests whether a small value weight improves the carry sleeve.
expected_failure_modes: correlated drawdown if value and carry both fail (2008)

name: Value-tilted blend (70/30 IRP/rate)
rationale: Value-dominant blend; tests whether reversion-led signal with a carry kicker is more robust in calm regimes than pure carry.

name: Carry, no momentum filter, lower threshold
rationale: Pure carry with momentum gate removed — isolates whether the momentum filter helps or hurts the carry sleeve specifically.
needs_factor: DUPLICATE_OF_CARRY-001 (same delta) — kept for documentation; loader dedups

name: Currency momentum (primary signal)
source: Menkhoff, Sarno, Schmeling, Schrimpf 2012b, Currency Momentum Strategies (JFE)
rationale: Past 1-12m FX returns predict future returns; momentum as a PRIMARY signal (not just a carry filter). Low correlation with carry.
needs_factor: momentum_primary (backtest only uses momentum as a confirmation filter, not a standalone signal)

name: PPP-deviation value
rationale: Real exchange rate (CPI-adjusted PPP) deviations mean-revert over multi-year horizons. ppp_z exists in the LIVE macro_engine but not the backtest signal.

name: Global FX volatility factor
source: Menkhoff, Sarno, Schmeling, Schrimpf 2012a, Carry Trades and Global FX Volatility (JF)
rationale: Innovations in global FX volatility price the carry cross-section; low-rate currencies hedge vol risk. Test as a sizing/timing gate on carry.

name: Dollar factor / dollar carry
source: Lustig, Roussanov, Verdelhan 2014, Countercyclical Currency Risk Premia (JFE); Verdelhan 2018 (JF)
rationale: Average-dollar exposure carries a time-varying premium tied to US-vs-rest rate gap and the global business cycle. A market-wide (not cross-sectional) factor.

name: Carry crash-risk hedge (skewness)
source: Brunnermeier, Nagel, Pedersen 2008, Carry Trades and Currency Crashes (NBER Macro Annual)
rationale: Carry returns are negatively skewed; conditioning on funding-liquidity / risk-reversal skew reduces crash exposure. Test as a carry overlay.

name: Yield-curve slope → currency
rationale: Relative yield-curve slope (term spread) between countries predicts currency returns via expected-policy-path differences.

name: Commodity-currency link (AUD-metals, CAD-oil)
rationale: Commodity-exporter currencies (AUD, CAD, NZD) co-move with their key export prices; commodity moves lead the currency.

name: Risk-on/risk-off (VIX → JPY/AUD)
source: Practitioner factor; cf. Brunnermeier-Nagel-Pedersen 2008 funding liquidity
rationale: VIX spikes drive safe-haven flows (long JPY/CHF, short AUD/NZD). A VIX-conditioned overlay already partially present as the VIX gate.
needs_factor: directional_vix_overlay (current VIX use is a suppression gate, not a directional signal)

name: External imbalances (NXA/current account)
source: Della Corte, Riddiough, Sarno 2016, Currency Premia and Global Imbalances (RFS)
rationale: Net-foreign-asset / current-account positions predict currency risk premia (debtor currencies earn more).

name: Order flow (microstructure)
source: Evans, Lyons 2002, Order Flow and Exchange Rate Dynamics (JPE)
rationale: Signed order flow explains a large share of short-horizon FX moves. Hard/impossible on retail data.
